mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a comprehensive Kubernetes distribution that provides a developer-friendly platform for building, deploying, and managing containerized applications. It includes additional features such as a web console, integrated CI/CD, and enhanced security. Key trade-offs include the complexity of installation and management compared to vanilla Kubernetes, but it offers a rich set of tools and support for enterprise environments.

steps.title: Install the OpenShift CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

```

> This command downloads the latest OpenShift CLI binary.
commands: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

steps.title: Create an OpenShift cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new OpenShift cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Replace 'mycluster' with your desired directory name.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=mycluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to your cluster's kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step includes optional configurations for autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-operator.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-operator.yaml

```

> This installs the cluster autoscaler.
> This installs monitoring components.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the OpenShift cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

```

> Replace 'mycluster' with your cluster directory.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

kubectl delete pvc --all

```

> This command deletes all persistent volume claims.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the OpenShift cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster. This includes draining nodes if necessary.

```bash

oc adm drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --force

openshift-install upgrade cluster --dir=mycluster --log-level=info

oc get nodes

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades your cluster to the latest version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates an issue with the kubeconfig or the cluster's API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure your KUBECONFIG is set correctly and matches the cluster's configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in <namespace>'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the specified namespace does not exist or if you are not authenticated correctly.

**Fix:**
Check your namespace with 'oc get namespaces' and ensure you have the correct permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the requested operation.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster by adding more nodes or adjusting resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image cannot be pulled from the container registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure that the image exists and is accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for Kubernetes 1.30, enhanced security features, and improved monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy authentication methods in favor of OAuth 2.0.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 4.16, ensure that all custom resource definitions are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes listed as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before starting the installation.
